name: aberdeen-1995-digital-objectbroker
title: Digital's ObjectBroker -- Advanced Integration of Distributed Resources
description: Aberdeen Group product profile (1995) evaluating Digital Equipment Corporation's ObjectBroker, a CORBA-compliant object request broker for integrating distributed heterogeneous resources across 19 hardware/OS platform combinations. Examines ORB architecture, legacy system encapsulation, OLE-COM integration, pricing, and competitive positioning.

sources.title: Aberdeen Group Product Profile: Digital ObjectBroker

resources.name: codes
resources.title: Reference Codes
resources.description: Controlled vocabulary of code types, values, and labels used across all tables in this data package. Includes study types, domains, methodologies, entity types, entity statuses, technology lifecycle statuses, platform codes, and observation categories.

fields.name: code_type
fields.description: Category of code (e.g. study_type, domain, entity_status)

resources.name: studies
resources.title: Studies
resources.description: Master study registry. Each row is one Aberdeen Group study. Contains bibliographic metadata, study type, domain, methodology, and source references.

fields.name: study_id
fields.description: Unique slug identifier for the study

fields.name: study_type
fields.description: Type of study (FK to codes.code_value where code_type=study_type)

fields.name: archived_date
fields.description: Date of Wayback Machine archive snapshot

resources.name: entities
resources.title: Entities
resources.description: Organizations and companies mentioned in the study. Includes vendors, standards bodies, systems integrators, and analyst firms with current status annotations.

fields.name: entity_id
fields.description: Unique short identifier for the entity

fields.name: entity_name
fields.description: Full legal or common entity name

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Entity type code (FK to codes where code_type=entity_type)

fields.name: role_in_study
fields.description: Role this entity plays in the study

fields.name: status_at_pub
fields.description: Entity status at time of publication

fields.name: status_current
fields.description: Entity status as of archival date (2026)

fields.name: status_notes
fields.description: Notes on status changes and acquisitions

fields.name: hq_location
fields.description: Headquarters location at time of publication

resources.name: technologies
resources.title: Technologies
resources.description: Technologies, standards, languages, products, and protocols mentioned in the study. Includes lifecycle status as of 2026 archival date.

fields.name: tech_category
fields.description: Category: middleware, standard, product, language, tool, etc.

fields.name: lifecycle_status
fields.description: Lifecycle status as of 2026 (FK to codes where code_type=tech_lifecycle)

fields.name: lifecycle_notes
fields.description: Notes on technology evolution or obsolescence

fields.name: related_entity_id
fields.description: FK to entities.entity_id for primary associated vendor/owner

resources.name: platforms
resources.title: Platforms
resources.description: All 19 hardware/OS platform combinations supported by ObjectBroker, including 15 available in v2.5 and 4 planned for v2.6. One row per platform.

fields.name: platform_code
fields.description: FK to codes.code_id for platform code

fields.name: port_by
fields.description: FK to entities.entity_id for organization responsible for port

resources.name: observations
resources.title: Observations
resources.description: Structured observations extracted from the study text. Covers pricing, product specifications, competitive position, customer segments, architecture, future roadmap, analyst assessments, and market context. 50 rows.

fields.name: obs_code
fields.description: FK to codes.code_id for observation category

fields.name: subject
fields.description: Short subject label for the observation

fields.name: value_text
fields.description: Text representation of any value or finding

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Extraction confidence: high, medium, or low
